title: EEG: Visual Working Memory + Cabergoline Challenge
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 27 healthy participants engaged in a visual working memory task during a double-blind, placebo-controlled pharmacological challenge with cabergoline (1.25 mg). Collected circa 2012-2013, the study employed a within-subject design with two sessions to investigate the neural correlates of working memory and dopaminergic modulation. Behavioral data from this sample have been previously published (10.3758/s13415-018-0584-6), and the same sample was used in a related published study (10.1038/ncomms6394). The EEG data remain unpublished and represent a novel resource for investigating electrophysiological mechanisms of cognitive control.
methods_description: Double-blind, placebo-controlled drug study with 27 participants (30 enrolled, 3 dropouts) completing two sessions. Participants performed a visual working memory task while EEG was recorded. Drug administration: Cabergoline 1.25 mg. Data collected circa 2012-2013 at the Laboratory for Neural Computation & Cognition at Brown University.
